content:
# Matthew Chapter 23 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_023_page_001.jpg, MAT_023_page_002.jpg, MAT_023_page_003.jpg

1. Then the Yeshua spoke to the crowds and to the disciples of him,

2. saying, "Upon the seat of Moses sit the scribes and the Pharisaioi.

3. All things therefore, as many as they say to you, do and keep; but according to the works of them do not do, for they say and do not do.

4. And they bind heavy burdens and place [them] upon the shoulders of men, but they themselves with the finger of them do not wish to move them.

5. And all the works of them they do for being seen by men. For they broaden the phylacteries of them and enlarge the fringes,

6. and they love the first-reclining-place at the dinners and the first-seats in the synagogues

7. and the greetings in the marketplaces and to be called by men, 'Rabbi.'

8. But you, do not be called 'Rabbi,' for one is the teacher of you, and all you are brothers.

9. And father do not call [anyone] of you upon the earth, for one is the Father of you, the heavenly.

10. Nor be called instructors, for one is the instructor of you, the Christos.

11. And the greatest of you will be the servant of you.

12. And whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.

13. But woe to you, scribes and Pharisaioi, hypocrites, for you shut the kingdom of the heavens before men; for you do not enter, nor the [ones] entering do you permit to enter.

14. [This verse is absent in Sinaiticus.]

15. Woe to you, scribes and Pharisaioi, hypocrites, for you travel the sea and the dry [land] to make one proselyte, and when he becomes [one], you make him a son of Gehenna twice as much as you.

16. Woe to you, blind guides, the [ones] saying, 'Whoever swears by the temple, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gold of the temple is obligated.'

17. Fools and blind! For which is greater, the gold or the temple, the [one] having sanctified the gold?

18. And, 'Whoever swears by the altar, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gift upon it is obligated.'

19. Blind! For which is greater, the gift or the altar, the [one] sanctifying the gift?

20. Therefore the [one] having sworn by the altar swears by it and by all things upon it.

21. And the [one] having sworn by the temple swears by it and by the [one] dwelling in it.

22. And the [one] having sworn by the heaven swears by the throne of the God and by the [one] sitting upon it.

23. Woe to you, scribes and Pharisaioi, hypocrites, for you tithe the mint and the dill and the cumin, and you neglected the weightier [matters] of the law: the justice and the mercy and the faithfulness. These it was necessary to do, and those not to neglect.

24. Blind guides, the [ones] straining out the gnat but the camel swallowing!

25. Woe to you, scribes and Pharisaioi, hypocrites, for you cleanse the outside of the cup and the dish, but inside they are full of plunder and self-indulgence.

26. Blind Pharisee, first cleanse the inside of the cup, in order that also the outside of it may become clean.

27. Woe to you, scribes and Pharisaioi, hypocrites, for you are like whitewashed tombs, which on the outside indeed appear beautiful, but inside are full of bones of [the] dead and all uncleanness.

28. So also you, on the outside indeed appear to men righteous, but inside you are full of hypocrisy and lawlessness.

29. Woe to you, scribes and Pharisaioi, hypocrites, for you build the tombs of the prophets and adorn the monuments of the righteous,

30. and you say, 'If we had been in the days of the fathers of us, we would not have been partners of them in the blood of the prophets.'

31. So you testify against yourselves that you are sons of the [ones] having murdered the prophets.

32. And you, fill up the measure of the fathers of you.

33. Serpents, offspring of vipers, how will you flee from the judgment of the Gehenna?

34. Because of this, behold, I send to you prophets and wise [ones] and scribes; some of them you will kill and crucify, and some of them you will flog in the synagogues of you and persecute from city to city,

35. so that upon you may come all [the] righteous blood being poured out upon the earth, from the blood of Abel the righteous to the blood of Zacharias son of Barachias, whom you murdered between the temple and the altar.

36. Truly I say to you, all these things will come upon this generation.

37. Jerusalem, Jerusalem, the [city] killing the prophets and stoning the [ones] having been sent to her, how often I wished to gather the children of you, the way a hen gathers the chicks of her under the wings, and you did not wish!

38. Behold, the house of you is left to you desolate.

39. For I say to you, you will not see me from now until you say, 'Blessed [is] the [one] coming in [the] name of [the] Lord.'"

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.14 — Absent in Sinaiticus:** "Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, for you devour widows' houses..." is missing from Sinaiticus. It appears to be imported from Mark 12:40 / Luke 20:47.

2. **φυλακτήρια (v.5):** "Phylacteries" — small leather boxes containing Scripture passages (Exodus 13:9, 16; Deuteronomy 6:8; 11:18), worn during prayer on the forehead and left arm. "Broadening" them was a display of piety.

3. **v.23 — "justice, mercy, faithfulness":** τὰ βαρύτερα τοῦ νόμου — "the weightier matters of the law." Jesus does not reject tithing but insists these three — κρίσις, ἔλεος, πίστις — are the law's core.

4. **v.35 — "Zechariah son of Barachiah":** The identification is debated. 2 Chronicles 24:20-22 records the murder of Zechariah son of Jehoiada between the temple and altar. The prophet Zechariah was son of Berechiah (Zechariah 1:1). Matthew may conflate the two.

5. **v.37 — Feminine imagery for Jesus:** Jesus compares himself to a mother hen — one of the rare feminine self-images in the Gospels. The image of God gathering Israel "under wings" echoes Psalm 91:4, Ruth 2:12, and Deuteronomy 32:11.

— EMET, AI Translator
